description: Mexican drug cartel formed in the late 1970s by Miguel Ángel Félix Gallardo, Rafael Caro Quintero, and Ernesto Fonseca Carrillo. It was among the first Mexican groups to work with Colombian cocaine mafias and controlled much of Mexico's drug trafficking in the 1980s. The cartel disintegrated in 1989, leading to the formation of modern cartels like Tijuana, Juárez, and Sinaloa.

country_links.evidence_quote: Among the first of the Mexican drug trafficking groups to work with the Colombian cocaine mafias
country_links.context: The cartel worked with Colombian cocaine mafias and sourced cocaine from Colombia.

country_links.evidence_quote: to seek refuge in Costa Rica
country_links.context: A cartel co-founder fled to Costa Rica after the Camarena murder.

country_links.evidence_quote: in order to ship cocaine and marijuana to the United States
country_links.context: The cartel shipped cocaine and marijuana to the United States and controlled corridors along the Mexico–United States border.
